title: Crotalaria brachycarpa
intro: Crotalaria brachycarpa is a species of flowering plant in the pea family, Fabaceae, native to tropical and subtropical regions. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and digestive issues, due to its potential health benefits. Bioactive constituents of Crotalaria brachycarpa include alkaloids, glycosides, and flavonoids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being incorporated into traditional remedies such as tonics and salves.
taxonomy: Crotalaria brachycarpa is classified within the plant family Fabaceae, commonly known as legumes. This family is further divided into the subfamily Faboideae, which includes many species of beans, peas, and other related plants. The genus Crotalaria is a group of flowering plants that are native to tropical and subtropical regions around the world. Crotalaria brachycarpa is specifically a species of shrub or small tree that is native to tropical Africa. It is part of the larger clade of flowering plants called Angiosperms, which also includes many other types of plants.
uses: Crotalaria brachycarpa, also known as the narrow-podded rattlebox, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. Its roots and leaves are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating wounds and infections. In some cultures, the plant is used to treat fever, cough, and other respiratory issues. The extracts from Crotalaria brachycarpa are also being studied for their potential antioxidant and anticancer properties. More research is needed to fully understand the medicinal uses and benefits of this plant.

side_effects: Crotalaria brachycarpa, a plant of the legume family, contains pyrrolizidine alkaloids, which have been reported to cause liver damage and other health issues in humans and animals. Prolonged exposure to these toxins can lead to symptoms such as jaundice, cirrhosis, and even liver failure. Ingestion of the plant's seeds or leaves can also result in gastrointestinal problems, including nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea. The toxic effects of Crotalaria brachycarpa are particularly concerning for livestock, which may experience weight loss, anemia, and other systemic problems after consuming the plant. It is essential to handle and manage Crotalaria brachycarpa with caution to minimize the risk of exposure to these toxic compounds.
